> [!NOTE]
> Substantial protocol changes go through an RFC in `spec/` before code lands.
> If this touches consensus, the runtime, receipts, or the fee market, expect to
> draft or amend an RFC. Read `spec/README.md` first — your idea may already be
> tracked.

## Problem

<!-- What is missing or painful today? Be concrete. -->

## Proposed change

<!-- What you would like to happen. -->

## Affected component

- [ ] Runtime / compute budget
- [ ] Consensus / slashing
- [ ] Decision receipts / ledger
- [ ] Priority-fee market
- [ ] Programs (`receipt-registry` / `hermes-faucet`)
- [ ] SDKs / CLI / `/v1` API
- [ ] docs / tooling

## Does this need an RFC?

- [ ] Yes — I will draft / amend one in `spec/`.
- [ ] No — it is additive and non-breaking. (Explain below.)

## Alternatives considered

<!-- Other approaches and why you did not pick them. -->
